title: AI Ethics Principles Published
description: Australia publishes its voluntary AI Ethics Principles, establishing 8 principles for responsible AI.

title: ADM and AI Regulation Issues Paper Released
description: The Digital Technology Taskforce launches consultation on automated decision-making and AI regulation.

title: Responsible AI Network publishes its first major report
description: Australia's National AI Centre, coordinated by CSIRO, publishes the Responsible AI Network's first major report on implementing Australia's AI Ethics Principles.

source.title: New report to help businesses implement responsible AI

title: Privacy Act Review Response Released
description: The Australian Government releases its response to the Privacy Act Review, including positions relevant to AI and automated decision-making.

verification.notes: Legacy timeline entry; its date and description require editorial re-checking against the source.

title: ASD launches Blueprint for Secure Cloud
description: ASD's Blueprint for Secure Cloud launches with security and governance guidance later updated to identify a general-purpose artificial intelligence usage policy as an organisational policy requirement under ISM control ISM-2074.

verification.notes: Legacy timeline entry; its date and description require editorial re-checking against the source.

title: Interim response to Safe and Responsible AI consultation
description: Government agrees to a risk-based approach to AI regulation and establishes a temporary AI Expert Group.

verification.notes: Legacy timeline entry; its date and description require editorial re-checking against the source.

title: WA AI Policy and Assurance Framework published
description: Western Australia publishes its whole-of-government AI policy and assurance framework.

title: Victorian Supreme Court issues AI guidelines for litigants
description: First Victorian Supreme Court guidance on responsible AI use in proceedings (County Court followed in July 2024).

verification.notes: Legacy timeline entry; its date and description require editorial re-checking against the source.

title: National framework for the assurance of AI in government agreed
description: Data and Digital Ministers agree a joint Commonwealth-state assurance framework mapped to the AI Ethics Principles.

title: NSW AI Assessment Framework Made Mandatory
description: NSW Circular DCS-2024-04 requires agencies using AI to apply the NSW AI Assessment Framework and AI Ethics Policy.

verification.notes: Legacy timeline entry; its date and description require editorial re-checking against the source.

title: ACT Government AI Policy published
description: The ACT publishes version 0.1 of its AI policy with a self-assessment tool (July 2024).

title: Queensland AI Governance Policy Published
description: Queensland publishes its whole-of-government AI governance policy under QGEA.

title: Mandatory Guardrails Proposals Paper Released
description: The Australian Government releases its proposals paper on mandatory AI guardrails for high-risk settings.

title: Voluntary AI Safety Standard published
description: Ten voluntary guardrails for organisations developing and deploying AI, published alongside the mandatory-guardrails proposals paper.

title: OAIC publishes twin AI privacy guidance
description: Guidance on developing and training generative AI models, and on using commercially available AI products.

title: ASIC Report 798 on AI governance
description: ASIC finds a governance gap between AI adoption and oversight in licensees ("Beware the gap").

verification.notes: Legacy timeline entry; its date and description require editorial re-checking against the source.

title: Senate Select Committee on Adopting AI reports
description: Final report makes 13 recommendations, including dedicated AI legislation.

verification.notes: Legacy timeline entry; its date and description require editorial re-checking against the source.

title: Victorian Generative AI Guideline Published
description: Victoria publishes its current administrative guideline for safe and responsible generative AI use in the public sector.

title: Privacy Act amendments assented
description: Privacy and Other Legislation Amendment Act 2024 introduces automated-decision-making transparency obligations commencing 10 December 2026.

verification.notes: Legacy timeline entry; its date and description require editorial re-checking against the source.

title: NSW Supreme Court Practice Note SC Gen 23 commences
description: Restrictions on generative AI use in NSW Supreme Court proceedings take effect (issued 21 November 2024).

verification.notes: Legacy timeline entry; its date and description require editorial re-checking against the source.

title: SA launches the nation-first Office for AI
description: South Australia establishes a $28m Office for AI to drive whole-of-government adoption and governance.

verification.notes: Legacy timeline entry; its date and description require editorial re-checking against the source.

title: Australian Government AI technical standard released
description: DTA publishes a lifecycle technical standard for AI systems in government.

title: Productivity Commission interim report on data and digital
description: Interim report floats a copyright text-and-data-mining exception and cautions against AI-specific regulation.

verification.notes: Legacy timeline entry; its date and description require editorial re-checking against the source.

title: Queensland Supreme Court Practice Direction 5 of 2025
description: Practice direction on accuracy of references responds to AI-generated citations; QAO also reports on managing AI ethics risks.

verification.notes: Legacy timeline entry; its date and description require editorial re-checking against the source.

title: NSW publishes Australian-first AI agents guidance
description: Digital NSW releases guidance for using agentic AI in government.

verification.notes: Legacy timeline entry; its date and description require editorial re-checking against the source.

title: Guidance for AI Adoption supersedes the Voluntary AI Safety Standard
description: The National AI Centre’s six essential practices (AI6) become the current federal voluntary guidance.

title: Attorney-General rules out a copyright TDM exception
description: Government will not introduce a text-and-data-mining exception; CAIRG re-tasked to consult on licensing models for AI training.

verification.notes: Legacy timeline entry; its date and description require editorial re-checking against the source.

title: APS AI Plan launched
description: Department of Finance launches the APS AI Plan: Chief AI Officers in every agency, the GovAI platform, and GovAI Chat trials from April 2026.

verification.notes: Legacy timeline entry; its date and description require editorial re-checking against the source.

title: Australian AI Safety Institute announced
description: Government commits $29.9m to establish an AI Safety Institute, standing up during 2026.

verification.notes: Legacy timeline entry; its date and description require editorial re-checking against the source.

title: National AI Plan Released
description: The Australian Government publishes the National AI Plan setting out its economy-wide direction for AI.

title: DTA AI Policy Version 2.0 Released
description: Digital Transformation Agency releases Version 2.0 of the Policy for the Responsible Use of AI in Government, introducing mandatory requirements for transparency statements, accountable officials, use case registers, and risk-based impact assessments.

title: Productivity Commission final report released
description: Final "Harnessing data and digital technology" report recommends regulating AI through existing frameworks; tabled 29 January 2026.

verification.notes: Legacy timeline entry; its date and description require editorial re-checking against the source.

title: SA courts generative AI guidelines take effect
description: Guidelines apply to steps in civil and criminal proceedings across South Australian courts from 1 January 2026.

title: Modernised NSW AI Assessment Framework launched
description: NSW Office for AI launches the new AIAF, aligned to the national assurance framework and the EU AI Act.

title: Victoria’s AI Mission Statement launched
description: Economy-wide AI strategy with six pillars, a $5.5m data-centre plan and an $8.1m AI career conversion program.

title: eSafety industry codes take effect
description: Codes covering generative AI services and AI companion chatbots become enforceable, with penalties up to $49.5m.

title: eSafety reports on AI companion risks to children
description: Report follows transparency notices to four AI companion providers; Character.AI adds age assurance, Chub AI geo-blocks Australia.

source.title: eSafety report: AI companions putting children at risk

title: Government responds to the Senate Adopting AI report
description: Response to the Senate Select Committee tabled, consistent with the National AI Plan’s existing-frameworks approach.

source.title: Government response to the Senate Adopting AI report tabled
source.publisher: Department of Industry, Science and Resources — news

title: ASIC Warns on AI-Powered Investment Scams
description: ASIC announces expanded takedown action against phishing and investment scam websites and warns that AI-generated videos, fake endorsements and targeted ads are making financial scams more convincing.

verification.notes: Legacy timeline entry; its date and description require editorial re-checking against the source.

title: ASD Issues Frontier AI Cyber Security Guidance
description: ASD publishes guidance on frontier AI models after Anthropic's Claude Mythos Preview announcement, warning that AI can accelerate vulnerability discovery and exploitation while also supporting defensive remediation.

verification.notes: Legacy timeline entry; its date and description require editorial re-checking against the source.

title: Federal Court issues GPN-AI practice note
description: First Federal Court practice note on generative AI in proceedings, with verification and disclosure duties.

title: APRA Calls for Step-Change in AI Risk Management
description: APRA publishes an industry letter warning banks, insurers and superannuation trustees that AI governance, assurance and operational resilience practices are not keeping pace with AI adoption and frontier-model cyber risks.

title: ASIC open letter on AI-era cyber resilience
description: ASIC writes to AFS licensees and market participants on AI-driven cyber threats.

source.title: ASIC writes to licensees on AI-era cyber resilience

title: Victorian Supreme Court Practice Note SC GEN 25
description: New practice note on AI use by court users replaces the 2024 guidelines.

title: OAIC consults on automated decision-making transparency
description: Issues paper on ADM transparency guidance ahead of Privacy Act obligations commencing 10 December 2026; submissions closed 15 June 2026.

source.title: OAIC opens consultation on ADM transparency guidance

title: FCFCOA issues Practice Direction on AI (PD-AI)
description: Federal Circuit and Family Court practice direction applies to proceedings commenced on or after 1 December 2025.

title: First new mandatory requirement under the AI in government policy v2 commences
description: Phased commencement of version 2 requirements begins; all requirements apply from December 2026.

source.title: DTA AI policy v2: first new mandatory requirement commences
source.publisher: digital.gov.au — AI in government policy hub

title: Government announces Australian AI Standards and establishes Office of AI
description: The Australian Government announces proposed national AI standards with mandatory energy, water, safety and copyright requirements for large AI data centres, and establishes an Office of AI in PM&C to coordinate implementation.

title: Government sets whole-of-government AI consumer safety priorities
description: The Commonwealth identifies five AI-safety workstreams covering a Digital Duty of Care, privacy reform, workplace safety, consumer-law responses and a federal automated-decision-making framework.

title: Senate opens inquiry into artificial intelligence and data centres
description: The Senate inquiry covers AI and data-centre regulation, government deals, and community, industry, energy, water and environmental impacts. The official inquiry page now sets submissions to close on 1 September 2026 and the committee to report on 16 November 2026.

source.title: Artificial intelligence and data centres – Parliament of Australia

title: Australia and Canada sign AI safety collaboration MoU
description: Australia and Canada signed a non-binding memorandum establishing technical cooperation between their AI safety institutes, including work on evaluations, standards, research and capability development.

source.title: Memorandum of understanding on artificial intelligence safety collaboration between Australia and Canada

browserCapture.notes: Controlled browser review of the official Department of Industry page after the hardened server-side retriever timed out. The displayed publication date, cooperation scope and non-binding legal status were checked.

title: Australia, Canada and India establish ACITI technology partnership
description: Australia, Canada and India signed the ACITI Partnership MoU, creating a joint working group covering AI adoption and workforce skills, AI investment ecosystems, policy and regulation exchanges, and AI risk mitigation.

source.title: Australia, Canada and India to strengthen ties on AI and emerging tech

browserCapture.notes: Controlled browser review of the official Department of Industry news page. The displayed 10 July 2026 date and the joint working groups AI remit were checked directly.

reviewedDate.notes: The official Department of Industry page displays 10 July 2026 and states the MoU was signed on that date.

verification.notes: Verified as a federal timeline milestone. The partnership spans AI and other emerging technologies and is not treated as a domestic policy instrument.

title: ANAO reports on AI governance at the Australian Taxation Office
description: The Auditor-General found the ATO's arrangements for adopting AI were partly effective and made seven recommendations on governance, risk management, evaluation and information management, all accepted by the ATO.

source.title: Governance of Artificial Intelligence at the Australian Taxation Office

browserCapture.notes: Controlled browser review of the official ANAO performance-audit page. The report number, publication date, conclusion and recommendation outcome were checked directly.

title: Digital secretaries clarify AI recordkeeping and FOI obligations
description: The Secretaries’ Digital and Data Committee confirmed APS accountability for AI records under National Archives guidance, commissioned practical recordkeeping and FOI guidance, and discussed frontier-model cyber risks and AI in government security operations centres.
